title: TUI action_quit may leave simulator state inconsistent
description:
File: python/src/lichen/tui/app.py
Lines: 280-285

The action_quit method doesn't properly notify the simulator of disconnection:

    async def action_quit(self) -> None:
        if self._receive_task is not None:
            self._receive_task.cancel()
        self._disconnect_sync()  # Just sets _radio = None
        self.exit()

When a node disconnects from the simulator without sending a proper disconnect message, the simulator may:
1. Continue routing packets to the disconnected node
2. Keep the node in its neighbor tables
3. Waste resources on the phantom node until timeout

The async _disconnect method (line 360-380) properly calls await self._radio.close(), but _disconnect_sync doesn't.

Impact: Simulator state inconsistency, potential for stale routing entries.

Fix: Either await the proper disconnect in action_quit, or ensure SimRadio.close() sends a disconnect notification in its __del__ method.
notes: Commit: 9bbe26743. Updated action_quit to properly cancel and await receive_task before closing radio connection and exiting. This ensures simulator cleanup via TCP close detection in node_server._cleanup_node.
close_reason: Fixed by ensuring receive_task is copied, nulled, cancelled and awaited before radio.close() in action_quit; mirrors on_unmount pattern for consistent simulator cleanup on quit. Verified code change in python/src/lichen/tui/app.py:286. No new issues found.
